sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Cagliari have shown mixed results in their last 5 matches, with 3 wins, 1 draw, and 1 loss, displaying decent attacking intent but struggling defensively with no clean sheets in 4 games. Bologna, on the other hand, are in strong form, remaining unbeaten in their last 3 matches, including a dominant 4-0 win, and showcasing consistent scoring ability with key contributions from players like Riccardo Orsolini.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last two encounters, Bologna have dominated Cagliari, winning both matches (2-1 and 2-0) with superior possession and clinical finishing. Cagliari have failed to keep a clean sheet against Bologna in their last 7 meetings, indicating a defensive vulnerability against this opponent.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Cagliari, sitting 11th with 8 points, have a balanced goal tally (6 scored, 6 conceded) but struggle with possession (43.2%, 17th in the league) and creating chances in the final third. Bologna, positioned 7th with 10 points, match Cagliari's goal stats but benefit from better recent momentum and key individual performances, giving them a slight edge.

sections.subtitle: Match Outlook
sections.text: Based on Sofascore's probabilities (Home Win 28%, Draw 35%, Away Win 37%) and Bologna's superior head-to-head record and form, the match leans slightly towards an away win or draw. Expect a tightly contested game with Bologna likely to capitalize on Cagliari's defensive issues, though Cagliari could exploit home advantage at Unipol Domus.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At half-time, Bologna lead Cagliari by a score of 0-1 at the Unipol Domus. The solitary goal came from Emil Holm in the 31st minute, capitalizing on a well-structured attacking move by Bologna. Cagliari have struggled to convert their limited opportunities, while Bologna have shown greater control and intent throughout the first 45 minutes. The game remains open, but Cagliari will need to dig deep to turn the tide in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance: Cagliari
halftimeSections.text: Cagliari have found it challenging to impose themselves in the first half, with significantly less ball possession and fewer entries into the final third compared to their opponents. Despite creating a notable opportunity, they failed to capitalize, missing a big chance. Their defensive structure has held up reasonably well, with several players contributing to clearances and interceptions. However, their inability to win possession in dangerous areas has limited their threat going forward. They’ve shown resilience in duels, particularly in the air, which could be a key asset in the second half if they can leverage set pieces.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance: Bologna
halftimeSections.text: Bologna have dominated much of the first half, maintaining a strong presence in the attacking third and creating consistent pressure. Their goal from Emil Holm reflects their ability to turn opportunities into results, and they’ve been clinical when it mattered. With a higher number of shots on target and more penalty area entries, they’ve kept Cagliari’s defense on edge. While their duel success rate has been lower, their tactical discipline and ball retention have allowed them to dictate the tempo of the match. They’ll aim to build on this lead in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw Bologna largely controlling the attacking momentum, with sustained periods of dominance early on and leading up to their goal. They had multiple spells of intense pressure, particularly around the middle of the half, which culminated in the decisive strike. Cagliari, on the other hand, showed sporadic bursts of intent, with a few moments of promise in the attacking third, especially towards the end of the half. However, their momentum often fizzled out, unable to match Bologna’s persistence and incisiveness in the final minutes.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players to watch
halftimeSections.text: For Cagliari, Matteo Prati has been a standout in midfield, creating a big chance and showing composure with tackles and interceptions. His ability to drive forward could be crucial if Cagliari are to mount a comeback. Mattia Felici also showed glimpses of danger with a shot on target, though he missed a significant opportunity. On Bologna’s side, Emil Holm has been the difference-maker with his goal and will likely remain a threat down the flank. Jens Odgaard’s contribution with an assist and key passes suggests he could play a pivotal role in creating further chances.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half expectations
halftimeSections.text: Looking ahead to the second half, Cagliari will likely push for an equalizer, potentially taking more risks and committing players forward. This could open up spaces for Bologna to exploit on the counter, especially with their attacking intent and ability to penetrate the penalty area. Bologna will aim to maintain control and look for a second goal to secure the result, while Cagliari’s focus will be on converting any set-piece opportunities or regaining possession higher up the pitch. The match could see a more open style of play as Cagliari chase the game.
